t_ch: 我找齐了这里的所有若虫。
t_en: I've found all the nymphs here.

t_ch: 真卷啊，贵客。那么现在，我也该完成自己的工作了……
t_en: So diligent, honored guest. In that case, it's time I fulfill my part of the deal.

t_ch: 金丝在人台周围凝聚，构成了一个包裹它身体的茧。
t_en: Golden threads gather around the mannequin, forming a cocoon that envelops its body.

t_ch: 当茧褪去时，唯余一块金丝织成的锦缎漂浮在空中。衣匠伸手一挥，它便分作两半，其中一半飞进了你的手里……
t_en: When the cocoon fades, only a piece of golden brocade remains floating in the air. The Garmentmaker waves a hand, and it splits in two, with one half flying into your grasp...

t_ch: 「让命运为你指点迷津——推石上山的若虫经历了多少次失败？」
t_en: "Let fate illuminate your path — How many times did the nymph fail to push the boulder uphill?"

t_ch: 这便是预言的内容…或许背后另有所指？
t_en: That is the message of the prophecy... Though perhaps, it holds a deeper meaning?

t_ch: 嗯…恭喜，贵客。你不需要我的帮助，也能解开预言的谜题——真是太酷了。
t_en: Well done, honored guest. You solved the riddle without any help from me. Now that's impressive.

t_ch: 能多给我点提示吗？
t_en: Can you give me more hints?

t_ch: 「让命运为你指点迷津——推石上山的若虫经历了多少次失败？」
t_en: "Let fate illuminate your path — How many times did the nymph fail to push the boulder uphill?"

t_ch: 前半句指的应该是雅努斯迷津？至于这后半句…至少答案应该是一个数字。
t_en: The first part likely refers to the Janus' Maze? As for the second... well, the answer's probably a number, at least.

t_ch: 我去忙了。
t_en: I'll get back to work.

t_ch: 玩得开心点，贵客。
t_en: Have fun out there, honored guest.

t_ch: 昔涟已经与你一同离开哀丽秘榭，开启了由你作为救世主的第<unbreak>33550337</unbreak>次回归。
t_en: Cyrene has already left Aedes Elysiae with you, beginning the <unbreak>33,550,337</unbreak>th return with you as the Deliverer.

t_ch: 仿佛是预感到未来的你仍会回到哀丽秘榭，在启程前，昔涟在桌上悄悄地留了一封信笺——
t_en: As if she sensed you'd one day return again, she quietly left behind a letter on the table, just before you left.

t_ch: 「哎呀，看来你还记得我说的话嘛：故事总会有戛然而止的时候。」
t_en: "Looks like you still remember what I said: Every story has to come to an end."

t_ch: 「而哀丽秘榭，就是你下一段旅程的起点。等准备好了，再从这里出发吧。」
t_en: "Aedes Elysiae is where your next journey begins. When you're ready, set out from here."

t_ch: 「虽然当你看到这封信的时候，我或许已经离开了这座村庄……」
t_en: "By the time you read this letter, I may have already left the village..."

t_ch: 「但我绝不会『消失』。我们曾在哀丽秘榭共同收获的回忆会代替我，继续陪伴着你……」
t_en: "But I will never 'disappear.' The memories we shared in Aedes Elysiae will stay with you in my place..."

t_ch: 「直到，我们再度重逢的那天♪」
t_en: "Until the day we meet again. ♪"

t_ch: 昔涟从海边找到的一枚缎带，看上去有些磨旧，缝埋着岁月的低语，还有少女的一句轻笑——
t_en: A ribbon Cyrene found by the sea, worn and weathered. It feels as if it was stitched together by the whispers of time and the soft laughter of a young girl—

t_ch: 「还记得我们玩捉迷藏的时候，我曾问过你：假如有一天，身边的人突然消失了，我们该怎么做呢？」
t_en: "Do you remember? When we were playing hide-and-seek, I once asked you: What would we do if one day the people around us suddenly vanished?"

t_ch: 「那时我没来得及和你分享，我心中的答案……」
t_en: "I never got the chance to tell you the answer inside my heart..."

t_ch: 「怀揣关乎身边重要之人的珍贵回忆，坚定地向前迈进，直到再度相会时，听到一句久违的问好♪」
t_en: "Cherish memories of those dear, and press ever forward. Someday, when we meet again, may it begin with a long-awaited hello. ♪"

t_ch: 迷！迷迷！绿球菜们，争点气迷！
t_en: Mem! Mem, mem! Come on, greenie cabbage, let's show some spirit, mem!

t_ch: 多喝露水，多晒阳光，长出翠绿脆嫩的叶片、结实饱满的菜心，不要被隔壁菜地的绿球菜比下去迷！
t_en: Soak up that dew. Soak up that sun. Grow crisp, green leaves and strong, plump hearts. Don't let the greenie cabbage next door outshine you, mem!

t_ch: 你在和蔬菜说话？
t_en: Are you talking to vegetables?

t_ch: 是啊，必须让绿球菜们加把劲才行迷。
t_en: Yeah, we've got to get those greenie cabbage to give it their all, mem.

t_ch: 压力给到绿球菜。
t_en: The cabbages are in the hot seat now.

t_ch: 我给它们加油鼓劲，是有原因的迷！
t_en: There's a reason I'm cheering them on, mem!

t_ch: 自从餐馆开张，果蔬谷物都供不应求了迷。绿球菜的价格，也迷地一下升高了不少！
t_en: Ever since the restaurant opened, demand for fruits, veggies, and grains has been sky-high, mem. Even the price of greenie cabbage has shot up like crazy!

t_ch: 嘿嘿…等绿球菜大丰收，我就能大赚一笔，天天去餐馆吃饭了迷~
t_en: Hehe... once the greenie cabbage harvest comes in strong, I'll be rolling in money and dining out every day, mem~

t_ch: 不过，那位采购商人精明又挑剔，从来只要品质最好的绿球菜。
t_en: But that merchant who buys? Sharp as a tack and super picky. Only the finest greenie cabbage makes the cut.

t_ch: 所以绿球菜们，加把劲，一定要被选中迷——想象自己在餐盘上，用脆生生的绿叶衬托菜肴…那是多么幸福的情景！
t_en: So come on, greenie cabbage, step it up and get picked! Picture yourselves on a plate, your bright green leaves making the main dish shine... What a glorious sight that would be!

t_ch: 到时候，我也会天天来看你们的迷！
t_en: And when that day comes, I'll drop by to see you every single day, mem!

t_ch: 站在你面前的，是妖精餐馆的终身会员——啦嘻嘻迷！
t_en: Standing before you now is a lifetime member of The Fairy Restaurant — Latitimem!

t_ch: 是高贵的会员！
t_en: A VIP member!

t_ch: 富贵使我们相遇。
t_en: Riches brought us together.

t_ch: 我没什么别的爱好，就是爱花钱，尤其是给中意的东西花钱。
t_en: I don't have many hobbies, but I do love spending money, especially on things I adore.

t_ch: 本来我想买下整个餐馆，可被那个看起来凶巴巴的餐馆主管直接回绝了。
t_en: At first, I wanted to buy the entire restaurant, but that fierce-looking restaurant supervisor shut me down instantly.

t_ch: 没办法，只好退而求其次，先办个终身会员了迷。
t_en: No luck, so I settled for a lifetime membership to the restaurant, mem.

t_ch: 可我还是想不通…怎么会有人面对十五颗半鹅卵石的高价，还能无动于衷迷？
t_en: But I still can't figure it out... How could anyone stay unmoved by a price as steep as fifteen and a half cobbles, mem?

t_ch: 怎么看新开张的餐馆？
t_en: What's your take on the new restaurant?

t_ch: 呵呵…贵客，哦不，应该叫你店长才对。最近生意还红火吗？
t_en: Hehe... My distinguished guest, oh no, I should call you manager. How's business these days? Still booming?

t_ch: 自我记事以来，这片密林还未如此繁荣过。
t_en: In all my memory, this forest has never been so full of life.

t_ch: 远远眺望餐馆的招牌，听着热火朝天的喧闹，看着原本漫无目的的妖精们找到了热衷之事…就像迷路迷境从悠长的熟睡中醒转过来了哪。
t_en: I can see the restaurant's plaque from a distance, hear the cheerful noise, and watch fairies who used to drift about aimlessly now find their passion... It's as if the Membrance Maze has finally awakened from a long slumber.

t_ch: 我知道，经营餐馆并不容易。可别太过辛劳哦，店长。
t_en: I know running a restaurant is no easy feat. Don't push yourself too hard, manager.

t_ch: 迷，白芋芋切块细致，大小均匀，火候掌握纯熟，摆盘也一丝不苟，勾芡食材的酱汁调和到了最佳比例……
t_en: Mem, the white taro is sliced with precision, every piece even in size, perfectly cooked, and plated with great care. The sauce tying everything together is blended to an ideal ratio...

t_ch: 看来这次是那位更谨慎的主厨掌勺迷。
t_en: Looks like it's the more cautious chef handling the kitchen this time, mem.

t_ch: 更谨慎的主厨？
t_en: A more cautious chef?

t_ch: 这也能尝出来？
t_en: You can tell?

t_ch: 是啊，可不要小瞧我的舌头。即便是同一盘菜肴，在不同主厨手中，口味也会完全不同迷！
t_en: Of course. Never underestimate my tongue. Even the same dish can taste completely different depending on who's behind the stove, mem!

t_ch: 至于另一位主厨…他刀法果断强硬，常常用大火翻炒，看来是听凭手感、信任直觉的豪放型厨师迷。
t_en: As for the other chef... his knife work is sharp and decisive, and he loves stir-frying over high heat. He seems like the type who cooks with instinct, guided by feel alone. A bold and free-spirited chef, mem.

t_ch: 你更喜欢他们哪一位呢？
t_en: Which one do you prefer?

t_ch: 迷迷…谨慎的主厨周全地考虑着方方面面，菜肴有种暖洋洋的感觉。豪放的主厨虽然手法相对粗糙，但也时常能带来惊喜……
t_en: Mem... The cautious chef considers every detail, and his dishes have a warm, comforting quality. The free-spirited one might be rough around the edges, but he brings surprises that leave a lasting impression...

t_ch: 真是好难抉择迷！
t_en: It's so hard to choose, mem!

t_ch: 迷…想我当年在外闯荡的时候，尝过比妖精耳朵还长的面包。一口咬下去，浓浓的谷香就像住进了嘴巴一样，好几天都不会散去迷。
t_en: Mem... back in my adventuring days, I once tasted a loaf of bread longer than a fairy's ear. The moment I bit into it, the rich scent of grain seemed to take up residence in my mouth and refused to leave for days, mem.

t_ch: 总觉得你们餐馆的菜肴，和当年我尝到的味道比还差些火候，究竟差在哪里了迷？
t_en: Somehow, I still feel that the dishes from your restaurant fall just a little short of that flavor I remember. What exactly is missing, mem?

t_ch: 是食材有差异吗？
t_en: Is it the ingredients?

t_ch: 是锅具有问题吗？
t_en: Is it the cookware?

t_ch: 是手艺不一样吗？
t_en: Is it the technique?

t_ch: 也可能，是我的心已经不复年轻了迷……
t_en: Or maybe... it's just that my heart is no longer as young as it used to be, mem...

t_ch: 都来都来，都来看看迷！多来多来，多来尝尝迷！
t_en: Come one, come all! Take a look, memi! Step right up, step right in, come and taste it, memi!

t_ch: 是店长迷，您需要来点什么吗？
t_en: Oh, manager, mem. What can I get for you?

t_ch: 我来看望看望大家。
t_en: Just dropping by to see how everyone's doing.

t_ch: 最近工作还顺利吗？
t_en: Has work been going well lately?

t_ch: 谢谢店长关心迷！能成为餐馆的服务员，每天端着热乎乎的菜肴，吃不限量的员工餐，是我从小的梦想迷。
t_en: Thank you for asking, manager, mem. Being a server here, carrying piping-hot dishes and enjoying unlimited staff meals every day, has been my dream since I was little, mem.

t_ch: 虽然真的当上服务员才发现，餐馆总是经营到很晚，一天下来脸都笑僵了，员工餐也常常忙得没空吃……
t_en: Though after actually becoming a server, I realized the restaurant stays open really late. By the end of each day, my smile feels frozen in place, and I'm often too busy to even eat the staff meal...

t_ch: 但把香喷喷的菜肴端到顾客面前，看着它们露出笑容，不知为什么，就脸也不酸了，肚子也不饿了，辛苦也都不算数了迷！
t_en: But when I bring a delicious dish to a customer and see them smile, somehow my face doesn't feel sore anymore, my stomach stops growling, and all the exhaustion just fades away, mem!

t_ch: 唔，店长，有顾客来了，我该去招呼招呼了迷。
t_en: Um, manager, a guest just arrived. I should go greet them, mem.

t_ch: 都来都来，都来看看迷！多来多来，多来尝尝迷！
t_en: Come one, come all! Take a look, memi! Step right up, step right in, come and taste it, memi!

t_ch: 哦，是店长啊。请站远一些， 汤汁溅到你身上就不好了迷。
t_en: Oh, manager, it's you. Please don't stand too close. I wouldn't want soup to splash on you, mem.

t_ch: 真抱歉，这里有些杂乱。这阵子顾客越来越多， 生意也越来越红火，真有点抽不开手了迷。
t_en: Apologies for the mess. Things have gotten a bit hectic lately. More customers are coming in, and business has really picked up. It's hard to find a spare moment, mem.

t_ch: 这阵子你辛苦了！
t_en: You've worked hard lately!

t_ch: 店长哪里的话，看着生意变好，我心里也好高兴迷！
t_en: Not at all, manager. Watching the restaurant thrive makes me truly happy, mem!

t_ch: 更何况，跟着店里的主厨打下手，我也学到了不少东西迷。
t_en: And working with the chefs has taught me so much, mem.

t_ch: 真希望有朝一日，我也能和他们一样独当一面啊！
t_en: I really hope that one day, I can hold my own just like they do!

t_ch: 你的名字好奇怪…
t_en: Your name is a bit unusual...

t_ch: 这个名字是我为自己起的，取自妖精中流传的一个传说迷。
t_en: I chose it myself. It comes from a legend among the fairies, mem.

t_ch: 传说有一种流淌在树干上的甜浆，像黄金一样金黄，像琥珀一样剔透，比迷境里任何果子都还要香甜，是代表着「永恒」的梦幻佳肴——
t_en: They say there's a kind of sweet syrup that flows along tree trunks. It's yellow like gold, clear like amber, and sweeter than any fruit in the Maze. A dreamlike delicacy that symbolizes eternity—

t_ch: 没错，那就是「哈基蜜」！在外面的世界，它似乎也被叫做「蜂蜜」。
t_en: That's right, that's "Hachimead." In the outside world, I think they call it "honey."

t_ch: 为了时刻提醒自己寻觅它的使命，我就以它为名了。是不是很好听迷？
t_en: To keep my mission of finding it close to heart, I took its name as my own. It sounds lovely, doesn't it, mem?

t_ch: 加油，不打扰了。
t_en: Keep it up. I won't bother you any longer.

t_ch: 店长不要这么客气，这是我哈基迷身为厨师应该做的迷！
t_en: No need to be so polite, manager. This is what I, Hachimem, am meant to do as a chef, mem!

t_ch: 迷！你好好擦桌子，等把手脚练得麻利些，我就安排你去端菜迷！
t_en: Mem, keep wiping those tables. Once your hands move fast enough, I'll have you serving dishes next, mem!

t_ch: 迷迷！你好好端菜，多揣摩揣摩顾客的喜好，等时机成熟，我就会安排你去当主厨迷！
t_en: Mem! Carry those dishes with care. Learn what the customers like. Once the time comes, I'll make you head chef, mem!

t_ch: 迷迷迷！你好好炒菜，等整家餐馆都离不开你的手艺，店长的位置就非你莫属了迷！
t_en: Mememe! Cook with heart. Once the whole restaurant depends on your cooking, the manager's seat will be yours, mem!

t_ch: 呃，那我怎么办？
t_en: Uh, what about me?

t_ch: 我好像要失业了…
t_en: Am I getting laid off...?

t_ch: 迷？！店长，你、你怎么也在迷！
t_en: Mem!? Manager, wh—why are you here, mem!

t_ch: 你、你的话…到时候当然早就已经荣升董事长了迷！
t_en: Y—You... by then, you'll have been promoted to chairman already, mem!

t_ch: 在餐馆庆祝真开心，真想每天都能过生日迷！
t_en: It feels so great to celebrate at the restaurant. I wish every day could be my birthday, mem!

t_ch: 有生日折扣，有惊喜点心，甚至员工还会围到桌子前，为我唱生日歌…听得我眼睛湿湿的。这才是过生日该有的样子迷！
t_en: There were birthday discounts, surprise desserts, and even the staff gathering around to sing to me... It made my eyes go all misty. That's what a real birthday should be like, mem!

t_ch: 服务员，给每桌都添壶水。就说是我请的迷！
t_en: Server, bring a pot of water to every table. Tell them it's my treat, mem!

t_ch: 为什么会这样呢…第一次抢到了优惠券，有了学生才能享受的六点九折折扣。两件快乐的事情交织在一起，本该给我带来更大的优惠。
t_en: Why did it turn out like this... It was my first time getting a coupon, and I also had a <unbreak>31%</unbreak> student discount. Two happy things overlapping should have given me an even better deal.

t_ch: 我本应获得梦幻般的用餐时间才对。但是，为什么，会变成这样呢……
t_en: I should've had a magical dining experience. But why... why did it end up like this...

t_ch: 为什么两种优惠不能叠加起来用迷……
t_en: Why can't I stack both discounts, mem...

t_ch: 大家好迷姆迷姆~这里是哆徕徕迷~
t_en: Hi everyone, mem-mem~ This is Doreremem~

t_ch: 妖精们，听说这家餐馆的菜肴味道正宗、价格实惠，分量特别大，能让妖精肚子瘪瘪地进来，肚皮鼓鼓地出去迷！
t_en: Fairies, word is the dishes here are full of flavor, budget-friendly, and served in huge portions. You'll come in with a flat belly and leave with a full one, mem!

t_ch: 迷境里的传闻究竟是真是假？神秘餐馆崛起背后的真相究竟如何？
t_en: Are the whispers from the Maze true or false? What's the real story behind this rising culinary mystery?

t_ch: ——请关注下期树叶小报的哆徕徕迷美食探访专栏评测迷！
t_en: —Tune in next episode on Leaflet News for Doreremem's food exploration review column, mem!

t_ch: 呵呵…你们在吃沙拉，但却对它的无穷奥妙一无所知迷。
t_en: Hehe... you're eating salad, but you know nothing about the wonders it holds, mem.

t_ch: 沙拉直接用叉子吃实在太过粗鲁。应该用菜叶卷起红酸果，一起送入口中迷。
t_en: Eating salad just with a fork is way too crude. Wrap the redsour fruit in leafy greens and savor them together, mem.

t_ch: 红酸果在齿间绽开时溢出的酸涩，融合绿球菜的清甜。仿佛清晨的第一滴露水在你的舌尖跃动，生机勃勃的绿意，由此开始在你的心里蔓延……
t_en: As the redsour fruit bursts between your teeth and melds with the greenie cabbage's sweet freshness, it feels like the first drop of morning dew dancing on your tongue. That vibrant green vitality begins to spread through your heart...

t_ch: 呵呵，下次有缘相会，再向你揭示美食的奥秘吧。
t_en: Hehe, next time fate brings us together, I'll reveal more culinary secrets to you.

t_ch: 呵呵…你们在吃烤饼，但却对它的无穷奥妙一无所知迷。
t_en: Hehe... you're eating pancakes, but you know nothing about the wonders it holds, mem.

t_ch: 将菜肴的香气封存进谷物之中，这天才般的想法，是迷路迷境何等的恩赐迷！
t_en: Locking a dish's aroma inside grains, what a genius idea! Truly a gift from the Membrance Maze, mem!

t_ch: 一口一口品尝会破坏精心营造的神秘感。迷地一口把烤饼全吞下去，在咀嚼中揭晓真相，才是正确的品尝方式迷！
t_en: Nibbling bit by bit ruins the carefully woven mystique. The right way is to eat the entire pancake in one go, and let each chew reveal the truth, mem!

t_ch: 呵呵，下次有缘相会，再向你揭示美食的奥秘吧。
t_en: Hehe, next time fate brings us together, I'll reveal more culinary secrets to you.

t_ch: 呵呵…你们在吃肉食，但却对它的无穷奥妙一无所知迷。
t_en: Hehe... you're eating meat, but you know nothing about the wonders it holds, mem.

t_ch: 让尖酸果汁流过肉的表面只是徒劳，将它滴入肉的纹理之间，才是品尝美味的奥义。
t_en: Letting the tangy fruit juice run over the meat's surface does nothing. Only when it seeps into the fibers of the meat can the true flavor be unlocked.

t_ch: 如此一来，仿佛溪流淌过干涸的河床，清泉填补每一个缝隙，每一口咀嚼，都能尝到肉经过提鲜后的滑嫩鲜香迷……
t_en: Just like a stream trickling through a dry riverbed, fresh spring water fills every crevice. With each bite, the tenderness and rich aroma of the meat unfold in full, mem...

t_ch: 呵呵，下次有缘相会，再向你揭示美食的奥秘吧。
t_en: Hehe, next time fate brings us together, I'll reveal more culinary secrets to you.

t_ch: 呵呵…你们在吃南瓜，但却对它的无穷奥妙一无所知迷。
t_en: Hehe... you're eating pumpkins, but you know nothing about the wonders it holds, mem.

t_ch: 起初，南瓜有着坚硬的外壳；但当你打开它，就能发现它的心灵竟是如此柔软。
t_en: At first, the pumpkin wears a hard shell. However, once opened, you discover a heart that's soft and tender.

t_ch: 经过一番缠绵的炖煮，南瓜与汤汁的边界模糊了，肉的鲜香和南瓜的醇香交织了…世间的一切善与恶、真与假，都仿佛在锅中混融为一……
t_en: After a slow and gentle simmer, the lines between pumpkin and broth begin to fade. The savory depth of meat and the mellow sweetness of pumpkin intertwine... In that pot, all the world's good and evil, truth and lies, seem to fuse as one...

t_ch: 怎么一直是你？
t_en: Why is it always you?

t_ch: 感觉你很眼熟。
t_en: You look really familiar.

t_ch: 呵呵，被认出来了吗？果然像我这样的美食家，在哪里都不能保持低调迷。
t_en: Hehe, have I been found out? As expected, someone like me, a true gourmet, can never go unnoticed, mem.

t_ch: 希望那些美食奥秘，能为餐桌前的你带来些许启迪的灵光迷。
t_en: May these culinary insights spark a little inspiration at your table, mem.

t_ch: 哈！叽里咕噜地说一大堆，不都是想劝我放弃。我偏不！
t_en: Ha! All that blah blah blah, you're just trying to make me give up. Well, guess what? Not happening!

t_ch: 什么「性价比」，什么「商业价值」，都是开公司要考虑的事，和我有什么关系？
t_en: Who cares about cost-effectiveness and commercial value? That's all IPC business stuff! What's it got to do with me?

t_ch: 路过的！你来说说——游戏想要好玩，最重要的东西是什么？
t_en: Hey, passerby! Tell me. What's the most important thing that makes a game fun?

t_ch: …引领时代的设计。
t_en: ...A design that leads the era.

t_ch: 哼，不懂了吧？优秀设计只是表象，你还没看到最核心的东西。
t_en: Hah, you don't get it, do you? Great design is just the surface. You haven't even seen the real core of it yet.

t_ch: …惊为天人的美术
t_en: ...Breathtakingly beautiful artwork.

t_ch: 哼，不懂了吧？游戏是用来玩的，光是好看还远远不够。
t_en: Hah, you don't get it, do you? Games are meant to be played. Looking pretty alone just doesn't cut it.

t_ch: …充足的开发资金。
t_en: ...Sufficient development funds.

t_ch: 哼，不懂了吧？钱就像是愿力，人人都有，但头脑之间亦有差距，以为有钱就什么都能办到，可大错特错了！
t_en: Hah, you don't get it, do you? Money is just like Wishpower: Everyone's got it, but there's a gap between minds. If you think having money means you can do anything, you're dead wrong!

t_ch: 等等，你凭什么考我？
t_en: Hold on, who said you get to test me?

t_ch: 哼，用问题来回答问题，现在的年轻人以为靠这个办法能让自己显得更聪明，殊不知，恰恰暴露了自己的无知！
t_en: Hmph, answering questions with questions... Young people these days think it makes them look smarter, when in fact, it only exposes their ignorance!

t_ch: 算了，就让我——本游戏制作仙人——来告诉你其中的诀窍。
t_en: Well then, let me, the divine sage of game developers, reveal the secret to you.

t_ch: 比巧妙的设计更根本，比华丽的美术更有吸引力，比鼓胀的钱包更能鼓舞人心，那就是「爱」啊！没有爱，是做不出来的！
t_en: More fundamental than clever design, more captivating than gorgeous art, more inspiring than a fat wallet... That's LOVE! Without love, it simply can't be done!

t_ch: 明白了？明白了就快来看看我做的游戏。嘿，保准叫你惊叹不已！
t_en: Got it? Now come check out the game I made. Hey, I guarantee it'll blow your mind!

t_ch: 老人颤巍巍地从口袋里掏出两架像素飞机，如至宝般捧到你的跟前。
t_en: With trembling hands, the old man pulls out two pixel planes from his pocket, holding them out to you as if they were precious treasures.

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」，最均衡的机体！各项数据都很优秀，擦弹判定宽松，绝招是超广范围结界，安定性高，适合任何玩家使用！我给它的评级是…「凶级」！
t_en: {RUBY_B#red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#}, the most balanced mech out there! Excellent stats across the board, forgiving graze hitbox, and its special move is a super-wide barrier. High stability makes it perfect for any player! My rating for this bad boy is... "Evil tier"!

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」，飞机中的战斗机！火力超强，威力超大，还有能让伤害倍增的绝技！缺点是移动慢，但在绝对的破坏力面前，这又算得了什么？
t_en: {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}, the fighter among planes! With devastating firepower, massive damage output, and a special skill that multiplies damage! Sure, it moves slow, but who cares about that when you've got absolute destructive power?

t_ch: 它的评级也是「凶」！顺便，还从未有「狂」及以上的机体出现过！
t_en: Its rating is also "Evil"! By the way, there's never been a mech rated "Crazy" or above!

t_ch: 哦。
t_en: Mmm-kay.

t_ch: 看你的反应，肯定很想玩吧？别骗自己，毕竟是旷世神作，正常人看到绝对都会被吸引的。
t_en: Judging by your reaction, you're dying to play it, aren't you? Don't lie to yourself, this is a masterpiece for the ages, after all. Anyone in their right mind would be drawn to it.

t_ch: 直接给我玩玩不就行了么？
t_en: Why not just let me play it?

t_ch: 哈，按捺不住内心的冲动了吧？能理解，毕竟是旷世神作，正常人看到绝对都会被吸引的。
t_en: Hah, can't contain your excitement anymore, can you? I get it, this is a masterpiece for the ages, after all. Anyone in their right mind would be drawn to it.

t_ch: 但是，我最喜欢做的事，就是对那些感兴趣的人说——「我拒绝」！
t_en: But you know what I love doing most? Telling interested people: "I refuse!"

t_ch: 我费尽心血、呕心沥血的作品，凭什么给别人指指点点？出再大的价钱都不行！
t_en: My blood, sweat, and tears went into this work. Who are they to criticize it? No amount of money in the world could make me budge!

t_ch: 不给玩是吧，那我走了。
t_en: Fine, if you won't let me play, I'm outta here.

t_ch: 你不只是仙人，更是神人。
t_en: You're not just a sage. You're a real piece of work.

t_ch: 等等，我没说不给你玩啊。看你打扮特立独行，和我中意的漫画角色有几分神似，必是游戏奇才。想玩对吧，帮我个忙就成。
t_en: Hold up, I didn't say you couldn't play. Looking at your unique appearance, I was reminded of a character from my favorite comic. You must be a gaming prodigy. You want to play, right? Just do me a favor first.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，我打算收徒，喊了几个人来面试。待会帮我看看，他们是不是有资质。
t_en: To be honest, I'm planning to take on some disciples. I've called a few people in for interviews. Help me check if they've got what it takes.

t_ch: 我这稀世罕有的手艺，要是传错了人可就糟糕了。
t_en: It would be a disaster if my one-of-a-kind skills fell into the wrong hands.

t_ch: 行吧，如果我有空的话。
t_en: Fine, I'll do it if I have time.

t_ch: 跟你说话真费劲…
t_en: Talking to you is such a chore...

t_ch: 我们约在游戏中心的门前见面。你要是闲得慌，就来看看。
t_en: Let's meet up in front of the arcade. If you've got time to kill, come check it out.

t_ch: 对了，还有个问题，我逢人必问的，你也给我老实回答——「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」和「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」，更喜欢哪个？
t_en: Oh right, one more question. I ask this to everyone I meet, so give me an honest answer: Between {RUBY_B#red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}, which one do you like better?

t_ch: …我选红的。
t_en: ...I'll choose the red one.

t_ch: …我选白的。
t_en: ...I'll choose the white one.

t_ch: 不给玩就不告诉你。
t_en: If you won't let me play, I won't tell you.

t_ch: 哼，我就知道，果然不该指望外行人能懂。你走吧，但别忘了我交代的事！
t_en: Hmph, just as I thought. Can't expect an outsider to understand. You can go now, but don't forget what I told you!

t_ch: 跟我念——不要因为喜欢8-bit游戏而感到羞愧！我就是我，我就是游戏制作仙人！
t_en: Say it with me: Never be ashamed of loving 8-bit games! I am who I am, the divine sage of game dev!

t_ch: 来到约定地点时，你意外撞见了一幕极为喜感的画面——
t_en: When you arrive at the meeting spot, you stumble upon an absolutely comical scene.

t_ch: <size=28>我不会因为喜欢8-bit游戏而感到羞愧！我要做游戏制作仙人！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I won't be ashamed of loving 8-bit games! I'm gonna be a divine sage of game dev!</size>

t_ch: 啧，再用点力！没气势怎么做游戏？你们有为游戏开发事业赌上一切的觉悟吗？！
t_en: Tsk, put some more effort into it! How can you make games without passion? Are you truly ready to put everything on the line for game development!?

t_ch: 算了算了，时间有限，直接来面试吧。就从你开始，你为什么想做游戏？
t_en: Alright, alright, time's short, let's get straight to the interview. Starting with you: Why do you want to make games?

t_ch: 我、我不知道呀。朋友说独立游戏制作人在网上很受欢迎，我就过来了。
t_en: I—I don't know. My friend said indie game developers are popular online, so I came here.

t_ch: 话说…旁边那两个在飞的，是你的幻造种？为什么打了马赛克？
t_en: By the way... those two flying things next to you, are they your imagenae? Why are they mosaicked?

t_ch: 啊，我知道了！一定是你在创造它们的时候，脑子里尽想些见不得人的东西，所以被自动屏蔽了！这就对了，嗯，都说相由心生嘛！
t_en: Ah, I get it! You must've had some naughty thoughts while creating them, so they were automatically censored! That makes sense. After all, your creations reflect what's in your mind!

t_ch: 你…你…不仅侮辱我的品格，还敢侮辱我的作品！快滚，别让我再见到你！
t_en: Y—You... you... not only insulted my character, but you dare insult my work! Get out, and don't let me see you again!

t_ch: 你戳到他痛处了。
t_en: You hit a nerve there.

t_ch: …说不定这就是事实。
t_en: ...That might just be the truth.

t_ch: 什么嘛，粉丝没几个，架子还真不小。这游戏制作人，我还不想当呢。
t_en: Geez, barely any fans but such a big ego. I don't even want to be the game producer anyway.

t_ch: 哼，连像素风都认不出，真是一代不如一代了。不和她多纠缠，你又是为什么？
t_en: Hmph, can't even recognize pixel art, kids these days are getting worse and worse. Forget about her, what about you?

t_ch: 我、我憧憬成为魔法少女！想要超强的火力，这样才能保护世界，交到朋友！
t_en: I—I dream of becoming a magical girl! I want overwhelming firepower so I can protect the world and make friends!

t_ch: 噢！我喜欢这个理由，我们肯定看过同一部动画！魔法少女，身边没几个仆从跟着怎么能行？很好，我认可你的梦想了！
t_en: Oh! I love that reason. We must have watched the same cartoon! What's a magical girl without a few servants by her side? Nice, I totally approve of your dream!

t_ch: 这就行了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 你们就是异父异母的兄弟。
t_en: You two are brothers from different parents.

t_ch: 噢！师父，我们赶快开始特训吧。我已经做好守护世界的准备了！
t_en: Oh! Master, let's start the special training right away. I'm ready to protect the world!

t_ch: 不错。以你的资质和热情，我保你十年出师，二十年获得最强称号。
t_en: Not bad. With your talent and passion, I guarantee you'll master it in ten years, and in twenty years you'll earn the certified title of the strongest.

t_ch: 那我什么时候才能成为魔法少女？
t_en: So when can I become a magical girl?

t_ch: 按经验推算，至少在获得头衔后十年吧。等等…你不是个男高中生吗？
t_en: Based on my experience, it would take at least ten years after getting the title. Wait... aren't you just a high school boy?

t_ch: 你做的游戏里，我没法随便按自己的意愿变身吗？
t_en: In your game, I can't just transform whenever I want?

t_ch: 不能。这个游戏里只有飞机，很多颜色的飞机。
t_en: Nope. This game only has planes. Lots of colorful planes.

t_ch: 没意思，看来这个办法也行不通呢。那再见啦，祝你们好运！
t_en: Boring, looks like this method won't work either. Well, catch you later. Good luck to you all!

t_ch: 哼，要么完全不懂，要么成天做梦，这种人怎么做得好游戏？我失望至极！
t_en: Hmph, either they're completely clueless or they've got their head in the clouds all day. How could someone like that make a decent game? I'm utterly disappointed!

t_ch: 你的要求太高了。
t_en: You're asking for too much.

t_ch: 神人是会相互吸引的。
t_en: Great minds attract each other.

t_ch: 不管那么多，现场不是还有个候选者么。你站到了最后，想必是真对游戏感兴趣吧？
t_en: Never mind all that, we still have a candidate right here. You stayed till the end, so you must be genuinely interested in the game, right?

t_ch: 我、我只是在发传单而已…店里有很多可爱的孩子，有兴趣来看看吗？
t_en: I—I'm just handing out flyers... There are lots of cute kids at the shop, would you like to come take a look?

t_ch: 大家玩游戏都很厉害，也看过许多老动画，知识丰富，肯定能陪您聊得开心！
t_en: Everyone here is great at gaming and has watched lots of classic cartoon. With such a wealth of knowledge, they'll definitely have a blast chatting with you!

t_ch: 我、我…办完正事就来光顾……
t_en: I—I'll... come by after I finish my business...

t_ch: 唉。我这稀世罕有的手艺，难道真要后继无人了吗？
t_en: *sigh* Will my rare and precious craftsmanship really die with me?

t_ch: 没了也挺好。
t_en: Good riddance.

t_ch: 不就是两架小飞机吗？
t_en: What's the big deal? It's just two little planes.

t_ch: 哼，你不懂，这可是我毕生愿力的结晶，怎么能随便糟蹋？我得替它找个好去处。
t_en: Hmph, you don't get it. This is the crystallization of my life's Wishpower. How could I let it go to waste? I need to find it a proper home.

t_ch: 不错，不错。从这位先生的言行中，确实能感受到满腹的才华。
t_en: Not bad, not bad at all. From this gentleman's words and actions, I can truly sense his abundant talent.

t_ch: 哦哦？你看起来呆呆的，眼神倒是不错！说话挺上道，正好我在收徒，要试试么？
t_en: Oh? You look a bit spacey, but there's something in those eyes! I like how you talk. Actually, I'm taking disciples right now. Wanna give it a shot?

t_ch: 哈，我是星际和平公司创意小组的负责人，日常职责便是在街头挖掘有潜力的艺人，看你言行如此奇特，想必是位难得一见的才子。
t_en: Ha! I'm the head of IPC's Creative Talent Division. My daily job is scouting potential artists on the streets, and seeing how... peculiar your behavior is, you must be quite the rare talent!

t_ch: 有兴趣加入公司吗？当然，是带着你的作品一起。
t_en: Interested in joining the IPC? With your work, of course.

t_ch: 啊？招安是吧？我呸！谁不知道被公司收购后是什么下场，想让我的游戏变成你们的摇钱树，休想！滚滚滚，跟那些噗噜说去吧。
t_en: Huh? Trying to buy me over? In your dreams! Everyone knows what happens after being bought by the IPC. You want to turn my game into your cash cow? Nice try! Get lost! Go tell that to those ploos instead!

t_ch: 还有闲心在这里嘴硬？那我问你，为什么急着收徒，不就是因为你自身的愿力，已经没法支持创作了么？
t_en: Still being stubborn here? Let me ask you this. Why are you in such a rush to take on disciples? Isn't it because your own Wishpower can't sustain your creative work anymore?

t_ch: 你、你胡说！我精神好着呢，通宵画图都不带心梗的！
t_en: N—Nonsense! I'm perfectly fine. I can pull all-nighters drawing without even risking a heart attack!

t_ch: 骗骗自己可以，别把旁边的这位{F#小姐}{M#小哥}也骗到了就行。要是你的愿力真那么充足，这俩小飞机早成了大妖怪，至于像这样摇摇欲坠的么？
t_en: You can fool yourself all you want, but don't drag this {F#miss}{M#mister} here into your delusions. If you really had that much Wishpower, these two little planes would've turned into mighty strangelings by now. Not barely staying afloat like this!

t_ch: 厉害的游戏我见多了，可简陋成这样，根本跑不起来的，哈，还是第一次见！
t_en: I've seen plenty of impressive games, but one this meager that can't even run? Ha, that's a first!

t_ch: 我会开出一个你无法拒绝的条件。很多事嘴上说说就得了，当真了，对谁都没好处。
t_en: I'll make you an offer you can't refuse. Some things are better left as empty talk. Taking them too seriously won't do anyone any good.

t_ch: 对吧，游戏老仙人？
t_en: Is that right, old sage of gaming?

t_ch: 别做仙人了，成为人类吧。
t_en: Stop trying to be a sage, just be human.

t_ch: 不要答应，这是资本做的局！
t_en: Don't fall for it, it's a capitalist trap!

t_ch: 那…你们会收购我的游戏吗，包括全部设定集、没完成的原画，还有废稿……
t_en: So... would you buy my game, including all the setting materials, unfinished key animations, and even the rejected drafts...

t_ch: 没问题。只要它们有足够商业价值的话。
t_en: No problem. As long as they have enough commercial value.

t_ch: 那…我、我…我愿……
t_en: Well... I—I... I would...

t_ch: 差点忘了。还有个事要你做，我不需要红色的飞机，改成白色吧。白色是大人气主播火花的应援色，肯定比其余颜色要卖得好。
t_en: Oh, almost forgot. There's one more thing I need you to do. I don't need the red plane anymore, change it to white. White is the fandom color for the popular streamer Sparxie, so it'll definitely sell better than other colors.

t_ch: 啊？但你不是才说，会收购我的全部游戏么？
t_en: Huh? But didn't you just say you'd buy every part of my game?

t_ch: 按需求进行修改，不就是开发者的工作吗？换个皮而已，对你来说应该很轻松吧？
t_en: Making changes based on requirements, isn't that what developers do? It's just a change of appearance, should be a piece of cake for you, right?

t_ch: 别叽里咕噜地说废话了，卖还是不卖，给个准信。
t_en: Cut the chatter and give me a straight answer. Are you selling or not?

t_ch: 决定前，我想…最后试飞一次。
t_en: Before I decide, I want to... take one last test flight.

t_ch: 搞创作的就是事多。行吧。别拖太久，我还赶着去看火花直播。
t_en: That's the problem with you creative types. Fine, whatever. Just don't take too long, I've got Sparxie's stream to catch.

t_ch: 你…对，你不是早就说想玩我的游戏么。最后这次机会，就让给你了。
t_en: You... yeah, you've been saying you wanted to play my game for a while now. Here's your last chance, I'm giving it to you.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>亲手摧毁自己的作品总是很残忍，替我做出选择吧。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>It's always too cruel to destroy your own creation. Please make the choice for me.</color>

t_ch: 好了。红色飞机都被打坏了，倒也省了换色的麻烦。你该想好了吧？
t_en: Alright. The red plane has been wrecked, saves us the trouble of repainting it anyway. Have you made up your mind?

t_ch: 拿去吧。你说得对，我的梦想不过是随便借个名字，用愿力信手画出来的涂鸦。
t_en: Take it. You're right, my dream was just a borrowed name, a doodle sketched with Wishpower on a whim.

t_ch: 但公司的财力，足以使这个美梦成真，成为玩家能玩到的、真正的游戏。
t_en: But the IPC has the financial power to turn this dream into reality, making it a real game that players can actually experience.

t_ch: 知道就好。走吧，我带你去办产权转移手续。往后，这可就是我们的创意了。
t_en: Good that you understand. Let's go, I'll help you with the property transfer paperwork. From now on, this will be our creation.

t_ch: 那边的{F#小姐}{M#小哥}，也谢谢你了。
t_en: That {F#miss}{M#mister} over there, thanks to you too.

t_ch: 喂喂，你在做什么？！不是说了要白色飞机么，白色！打坏了还怎么卖啊？
t_en: Hey hey, what are you doing!? Didn't I say we need white planes? WHITE! How are we supposed to sell them if they're damaged!?

t_ch: 哈。干得漂亮！高高在上是吧，叫我做换皮游戏是吧，那就别怪我们掀桌子了！
t_en: Ha! Well played! Acting all high and mighty, huh? Telling us to rebrand the game, huh? Don't blame us when we flip the table then!

t_ch: 但你的行为不会给公司带来任何损失，顶多是浪费我的时间。出于报复心而毁掉自己的作品，真的值得吗？
t_en: But your actions won't cause any harm to the IPC. At most, you're just wasting my time. Is it really worth destroying your own work, just for the sake of revenge?

t_ch: 什么值不值？那是你们商人的想法。做游戏，最重要的就是开心。
t_en: Worth? That's just how you merchants think. When it comes to making games, the most important thing is to make myself happy.

t_ch: 现在我开心了，看到你不开心，我变得更开心了，还有什么比这更值得的？
t_en: Now I'm happy, and seeing you unhappy makes me even happier. What could be more worthwhile than that?

t_ch: 我就是我！我是——游戏制作仙人！
t_en: I am who I am! The divine sage of game dev!

t_ch: 嗯。这游戏看起来还有点意思，就这么埋没掉可惜了，顺手帮个忙吧。
t_en: Hmm. This game looks kinda interesting. Would be a shame to let it go to waste. Might as well lend a hand.

t_ch: 加点炫酷特效，再补个激烈的对战画面，视频上传…搞定。
t_en: Add some cool effects, throw in an intense battle scene, upload the video... Done.

t_ch: 你可得认真点做，发售后，也给我玩玩吧。
t_en: You better take this seriously. After it's released, let me play it too.

t_ch: 哈？你就这么直接回来了，我交代你做的事呢？
t_en: Huh? You just came back like that? What about the task I gave you?

t_ch: 这就是我的选择。
t_en: This is my choice.

t_ch: 别推卸责任，你自己决定吧。
t_en: Don't shirk the responsibility. Make your own decision.

t_ch: 哼。倒还挺会说的。是啊，这是我该做的选择，和任何人都没有关系。
t_en: Hmph. You've got a way with words. Yeah, this is my choice to make, and it's nobody else's business.

t_ch: 红白两色的小飞机沿着喧闹的街道渐行渐远，越飞越高，直至消失在视野的尽头。
t_en: The red and white planes glided along the bustling street, climbing higher and higher until they vanished into the distant horizon.

t_ch: 说到底，游戏就是游戏。既不是某人的作品，更不是商品。<color=#dbc291ff>飞机，必须能自由地飞才行啊。</color>
t_en: At the end of the day, a game is just a game. It's not someone's masterpiece, let alone a commodity. <color=#dbc291ff>A plane needs to fly free, that's what it's meant to do.</color>

t_ch: 无聊。飞得再好，得有人看见才行。没有愿力支持，再完美的设计也是白搭。
t_en: Boring. No point in being a great pilot if no one's there to see it. Without Wishpower to back it up, even the most perfect design is useless.

t_ch: 这就是二相乐园的生存法则。谁都逃不掉。浪费我的时间，走了。
t_en: That's just how things work in Planarcadia. No one can escape it. You're wasting my time, I'm out of here.

t_ch: …会有人看见的。会有人喜欢的。
t_en: ...Someone will see it. Someone will like it.

t_ch: <size=28>「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」和「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」——你们一定要幸福啊！</size>
t_en: <size=28>{RUBY_B#Red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}! May you both find happiness!</size>

t_ch: 差不多得了。
t_en: That's enough.

t_ch: 两架飞机而已，至于么？
t_en: It's just two planes, what's the big deal?

t_ch: 哼，你不懂。小飞机？游戏？那可是我回不去的青春！
t_en: Hmph, you don't get it. Toy planes? Games? Those were the good old days I can never return to!

t_ch: 不过，谢谢你啊。最终，没有打碎它们。
t_en: Thank you anyway, for not breaking them in the end.

t_ch: 嗯。这游戏看起来还有点意思，就这么埋没掉可惜了，顺手帮个忙吧。
t_en: Hmm. This game looks kinda interesting. Would be a shame to let it go to waste. Might as well lend a hand.

t_ch: 加点炫酷特效，再补个激烈的对战画面，视频上传…搞定。
t_en: Add some cool effects, throw in an intense battle scene, upload the video... Done.

t_ch: 你可得认真点做，发售后，也给我玩玩吧。
t_en: You better take this seriously. After it's released, let me play it too.

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」和「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」，你会选哪个？
t_en: Which would you choose? {RUBY_B#red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#} or the {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 最后的这次试玩机会，就让给你了啊。你…对，就是你，替我做出选择吧。
t_en: I'll let you have the last chance to test it out. You... Yes, you. Make the decision for me.

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」和「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」，你会选哪个？
t_en: Which would you choose? {RUBY_B#red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#} or the {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: （想想该保留哪架飞机）
t_en: (Contemplate which plane you should keep)

t_ch: 嗯，那就好好想吧。我已经没有指手画脚的权力了。
t_en: Yeah, think over it carefully. I don't have the right to criticize you anymore.

t_ch: …我都不想打碎。
t_en: ... I don't want to break either one.

t_ch: 红色飞机是不需要的，只保留白色就好。对，那可是火花的应援色啊。
t_en: No need for the red plane, just keep the white one. Yeah, that's Sparxie's fandom color.

t_ch: （想想该保留哪架飞机）
t_en: (Contemplate which plane you should keep)

t_ch: 有什么好犹豫的。公司只需要白色，这个好卖钱，就这么简单。啧，想不通吗？
t_en: What's there to hesitate over? The IPC only needs the white one. It's lucrative, as simple as that. Tch, don't you get it?

t_ch: …我都不想打碎。
t_en: ... I don't want to break either one.

t_ch: 准点打卡，免费食堂，从不加班，年假拉满。嗨，做游戏哪有为公司卖命强啊！
t_en: Clock in on time, free food, no overtime, and plenty of annual leaves. Ah, I'm better off working for the IPC than making games!

t_ch: 诶诶，你来了。我打听个事啊——你有在市面上看到我做的游戏吗？
t_en: Oh, you're here. Let me ask you, have you ever seen the game I made in the market?

t_ch: 好像有？
t_en: I think so?

t_ch: 游戏太多了，我记不住。
t_en: There are too many games out there, I can't remember.

t_ch: 行吧。多的我也不问。有人负责发行那些杂事，我只管拿提成的那份就好。
t_en: Alright then, I won't ask for details. Someone else is distributing those things. I'll just take the commission.

t_ch: ——我啊，正过着神仙般的生活！
t_en: I'm living my best life!

t_ch: ——不要因为喜欢8-bit游戏而感到羞愧！我就是我，我就是游戏制作仙人！
t_en: Never be ashamed of loving 8-bit games! I am who I am, the divine sage of game dev!

t_ch: 哎，喊了老半天，也没来几个人。这个徒，还收不收得到了？疾锋龙列车终会到站，那我呢，我的游戏呢？
t_en: *sigh* Barely anyone came, despite all that shouting. Will I really be able to recruit a disciple? The Bullet Wyrm Express will eventually reach its destination. But, what about me? What about my game?

t_ch: 啊啊…不管！活到老，做到老！要是没人来，我就自个儿做！
t_en: Ah, whatever! I'm going to devote the rest of my life to this! If no one comes, I'll do it myself!

t_ch: 你看，捡到的这两架飞机模样好奇怪，旧旧的，丑丑的，早就没人喜欢这种款式了。
t_en: Look, these two airplane models I picked up look so strange. They're old and ugly, nobody likes this sort of design anymore.

t_ch: 那你为什么一直带着它，像是宝物那样呢？
t_en: Then why do you carry them with you, as if they're some sort of treasure?

t_ch: 想学吗，我教你们啊。
t_en: Wanna learn? I can teach you.

t_ch: 别磨磨蹭蹭的，走，我带你去办产权转移手续。往后，这可就是我们的创意了。
t_en: Stop dawdling, come on, I'll help you with the property transfer paperwork. From now on, this will be our creation.

t_ch: 啧，这么着急干什么，我又不反悔。最后，让我再和这些残骸多呆会吧。
t_en: Tsk, what's the rush? I'm not going back on my word. Just... let me stay with these wreckage a little longer.

t_ch: 你是做游戏把脑子做坏了？这两架飞机可是幻造种，地上干干净净的，哪来的残骸？
t_en: Did all that game development fry your brain? Those two planes are imagenae, and the ground is spotless. What wreckage are you talking about?

t_ch: 哼，你是真不懂。那个…是我梦想的残骸啊！
t_en: Hmph, you really don't get it. That... that's the wreckage of my dreams!

t_ch: 做游戏啊，最重要的就是开心。
t_en: When it comes to making games, having fun is what matters most.

t_ch: 哪怕飞机被打坏了，但只要我还在，我的独立精神还在！就总能重新做出来。
t_en: Even if the aircraft gets wrecked, as long as I'm still here, my independent spirit lives on! I can always build it again.

t_ch: 但现在我很开心，看到那个公司组长不开心，我更开心了，还有什么比这值得的？
t_en: But now I'm happy, and seeing that IPC supervisor unhappy makes me even happier. What could be more worthwhile than that?

t_ch: 我就是我！我是——游戏制作仙人！
t_en: I am who I am! The divine sage of game dev!

t_ch: <size=28>「{RUBY_B#红色飞机}梦灵号{RUBY_E#}」和「{RUBY_B#白色飞机}理魔号{RUBY_E#}」——你们一定要幸福啊！</size>
t_en: <size=28>{RUBY_B#Red plane}The Dreamsoul{RUBY_E#} and {RUBY_B#white plane}The Demonic Wit{RUBY_E#}! May you both find happiness!</size>

t_ch: 我会为你们，还有这个不讲道理的世界，献上我全部的爱和祝福！自由地飞吧！
t_en: I'll give all my love and blessings to you and this unreasonable world! Fly free!

t_ch: 你到底懂不懂规矩？
t_en: Do you understand the rules or not?

t_ch: 哪儿来那么多规矩！
t_en: Since when were there so many rules?

t_ch: 所以结论显而易见——摆盘时需要尽量堆高。越高，代表这道菜越有可能触碰到艾格勒的眼眸。
t_en: So, the conclusion is obvious... When filling your plate, you need to stack it as high as possible. The higher it is stacked, the more likely it is that your dish will catch the gaze of Aquila.

t_ch: 不，瑟希斯首要推崇的一定是颜色搭配，或许参照{RUBY_B#花花草草}裂分神迹{RUBY_E#}会更合理。
t_en: No! I am certain that Cerces would place the most importance on color coordination. Maybe it would be best to reference the {RUBY_B#Plants and Flowers}Rift of Miracles{RUBY_E#}?

t_ch: 我觉得，做菜，味道也很重要。
t_en: I think that flavor is also very important when cooking.

t_ch: 什么！瑟希斯听完都要枯了！菜品不考虑外观却注重味道？这不是本末倒置吗！
t_en: What!? Cerces would wither just hearing you say that! Placing the priority in a dish on flavor over appearance? Do you not think that's backwards?

t_ch: …不，大块头的意见或许有一定道理。味道也是一种艺术判断标准，这是被我们一直忽视的思路。
t_en: ...No, the big guy might actually be onto something. Flavor is also a criterion that can be used to judge art. This is a thought that we have all overlooked.

t_ch: 从头理一下吧，这次从餐具形状开始说起，正好{RUBY_B#讨论组}辩论场{RUBY_E#}也可以同步更新一下……
t_en: Let's go through this again from the top. This time, let's start with the shape of the cutlery. Fortunately, we can update the {RUBY_B#Discussion group}Forum{RUBY_E#} at the same time.

t_ch: 都说了——先热水、再温水、最后才是冷水！你怎么就死活不开窍呢！
t_en: I told you: Hot first, then warm, and then finally cold! How can you not get that?

t_ch: 能洗干净得了呗，哪儿来那么多讲究？
t_en: Being clean is good enough. Why do you have to be so fussy about it?

t_ch: 当然要讲究：热水清洁身体、净化魂灵；温水消除疲劳、放松心情；冷水冷却降温、振奋精神——
t_en: Of course I'm fussy about it: Hot water detoxes the body and cleanses the soul. Warm water eliminates fatigue and relaxes the mind. Cold water lowers the temperature and perks up the spirit...

t_ch: 只有这样，才能充分享受泉水，也才能展现你对法吉娜的敬畏，懂吗？
t_en: This is the only way to fully appreciate the spring water, and demonstrate your reverence for Phagousa. Get it?

t_ch: 懂了。但是不好意思，我信艾格勒。
t_en: I get it. But I'm afraid my faith lies with Aquila.

t_ch: 你这漂亮的小脑袋瓜究竟怎么长的？里头不会比陶罐还空吧？
t_en: What's wrong with that pretty little head of yours? Don't tell me it's as hollow as a dolium inside?

t_ch: 我错了……
t_en: I'm sorry...

t_ch: 每次不是忘带浴巾浴盐就是落下酒水餐饭，就你这记性还好意思在「花园」工作？
t_en: You either forget the bath salts and towels, or you forget the snacks and drinks. You've got some nerve, working at the Garden with a memory like that.

t_ch: 我真的错了…下次一定记得带…
t_en: Really, I'm sorry... I won't forget things next time...

t_ch: 算了，之前那些我也懒得跟你计较。但我问你，昨天新买的香氛呢？
t_en: Forget it. I don't want to bring all those old mistakes up now. But let me ask you this: Where's the new perfume from yesterday?

t_ch: …呃…我这就给你再买……
t_en: ...Uh... I'll buy you another right now...

t_ch: 唉，没见你多能赚钱，败家倒是一把好手！
t_en: *sigh* You're no good at earning money, but you sure know how to spend it!

t_ch: …也就是从那时起，我开始察觉——这「世界」或许并非我们所感知到的那般真实，或许希帕索斯是对的…
t_en: ...That was when I began to realize... This "world" might not be as real as we perceive it to be. Perhaps it's like Hippasus said...

t_ch: …不是，我又怎么得罪你了？干嘛突然拿冷水泼我…
t_en: ...Have I offended you again? Why are you suddenly pouring cold water on me...

t_ch: 水？哦，当然是水！正如神谕者泰勒斯所言：「水容纳一切又连接一切」。处在水中时，人们的思想便得以向外延展，感知更多……
t_en: Water? Oh, sure — water! It's just as the sage Thales first said: "Water constitutes the principle of all things and connects all." In the water, the mind stretches out further away and perception is greatly strengthened...

t_ch: 道理我都懂，但这和你把水泼我头上有什么关系？
t_en: I get the theory, but what does any of that have to do with you pouring water on my head?

t_ch: 人要想认识世界，就得先认识自己在世界中的位置——我这是在帮你疏通「内」与「外」的联系。不然就以你那远不及胸肌发达的脑子，怎么继续跟我交流？
t_en: If you want to understand this world, you have to first understand your place in the world — I'm helping you clear the way to connect the "internal" with the "external." Otherwise, how could someone like you with more brawn than brains be able to keep up with me?

t_ch: 你有病吧？！！
t_en: What's wrong with you!?

t_ch: 听说，为了犒赏黄金裔的英勇，上层浴场的水质如琼，秘酿管够！还有专业的辩论师供他们消磨时间……
t_en: I hear that, to reward the Chrysos Heirs' heroics, the water quality of the upper floor's baths is akin to liquid jade, with a free flow of ambrosia! They'll also be entertained by a professional debater...

t_ch: 真好啊，大哥，我也想当黄金裔。
t_en: That sounds awesome, man. I wanna be a Chrysos Heir too.

t_ch: 我怀疑，不可能单单凭运气。那些黄金裔一开始也是普通人，他们一定掌握了某种不为人知的方法……
t_en: I suspect it's not just sheer luck that'll get you there. Those Chrysos Heirs were all ordinary people when they were born. They must have grasped some secret method to gain that status...

t_ch: 我拿到情报了，想成为黄金裔不难。
t_en: I've got the intel. It's not hard to become a Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 不愧是你。
t_en: As expected of you.

t_ch: 这样我们就也能被当成英雄了吧！还能和阿格莱雅大人说上话……
t_en: That means we can be heroes too, right!? And we'll get to rub shoulders with Lady Aglaea...

t_ch: 瞧你那出息。总之，得赶紧做准备了，明后天就行动。
t_en: Is that the extent of your ambition in your noggin? Anyway, we have to prep quickly. We're moving out in a couple of days.

t_ch: 人们说情爱会使人盲目，而这份黯淡却恰与灿烂的永夜相称。若您愿接受一份典雅的热情，还请…回应我的心意。
t_en: People say love renders us blind, but this dimness is the perfect ambience that complements the shimmering night sky. If you would accept a passion of elegance, please... respond to my affection.

t_ch: 看来，我也不行啊…无论如何，愿您…再次寻得良缘。但还请记得，您有一位坚定的支持者，名为仄菲克。
t_en: Looks like I'm unworthy as well... No matter. May you... find happiness elsewhere. But please remember that you have an ardent supporter in me, he who is named Chefik.

t_ch: 离去吧，不要占用女士太多时间。温柔的女士，请允许本人自我介绍…
t_en: Don't waste a lady's time and take your leave. O gentle maiden, please allow me to introduce myself...

t_ch: 所以结论显而易见——摆盘时需要尽量堆高，越高，代表这道菜越能触碰到艾格勒的眼眸。
t_en: So, the conclusion becomes obvious: While being served, the food must be piled up as high as possible — the higher it is, the better it could feast Aquila's eyes.

t_ch: 不，瑟希斯菜式的重点一定是颜色搭配，或许参照{RUBY_B#花花草草}裂分圣迹{RUBY_E#}会更合理。
t_en: No, the focus of Cercesian cuisine must be the arrangement of colors. Perhaps something like {RUBY_B#plants}the Relics of Rift{RUBY_E#} is more reasonable.

t_ch: 开玩笑，要想让菜肴的艺术价值更上一层楼，材料的搭配才是重中之重。
t_en: As if. If you want to take your culinary craft up a notch, it's the combination of ingredients that is most critical.

t_ch: 想象一下，以{RUBY_B#刻法勒的食材}奥赫玛麦{RUBY_E#}作为基底做粥，加入十一位泰坦的对应食材，那道菜该有多么出色啊！
t_en: Just imagine, using {RUBY_B#Kephalean ingredients}Okheman oats{RUBY_E#} as a base for porridge, then adding in corresponding ingredients for each of the eleven other Titans — how amazing that would taste!

t_ch: 「吉奥里亚的食材」不是大地兽吃的吗？你想让黄金裔吃土？
t_en: Georios' Ingredients — aren't they food for dromases? You want the Chrysos Heirs to eat dirt?

t_ch: 那又如何？艺术性高于一切。
t_en: So what? Artistry is above all else.

t_ch: 我们扣握双手，向塞纳托斯颂祷，缘引他们迎来的，「第二次的逝亡」。
t_en: We clasp our fingers intertwined, singing hymns to Thanatos, to guard the fate they welcomed, "the second demise."

t_ch: 这是哀悼，也是祝福，所为他们领受的，「所有人的遗忘」。
t_en: This is both lament and blessing, for what they have received, is "being forgotten by all."

t_ch: 啊啊，曾几何时的有名之人。已然忘却的无名之魂。
t_en: Ahh, once individuals of renown, now nameless souls lost to memory.

t_ch: 愿你们迎来安详。
t_en: May you embrace peace.

t_ch: 愿你们被人回望。
t_en: May you be remembered.

t_ch: 至终，至创世，重新流淌。
t_en: In the end, at the dawn of genesis, flowing once more.

t_ch: 盲信会带来许多危害，试想，你因为一句不知真假的消息，便轻易下了某种草率的决定。结果呢？损失，后悔，还会让亲近的人看笑话。
t_en: Blind faith can bring many dangers. Just imagine, if you made a rash decision based on unverified information, how will things end? You will suffer loss and regret, and become a joke to your loved ones.

t_ch: 是啊，前两天我就信了他们说的，说什么艾格勒大人会因悲伤而哭泣，让我买了不少防雨防灾的东西，一个都用不上。
t_en: Yes. Two days ago, I believed what they said about Lord Aquila crying tears of sadness, and they got me to buy up all kinds of wet weather gear. But it turned out I don't need any of it.

t_ch: 你看看，你看看…多不应该啊。没关系，我正要教你们怎么杜绝这种情况。
t_en: See? See? That is what I'm talking about. Don't worry. I can just teach you how to protect yourselves from fraud for good.

t_ch: 拿着这个护身符，它会保护你们，让你们免受迷信的侵扰。
t_en: Take this Lucky Charm: It will protect you all from the pervasive reach of superstition.

t_ch: 哦哟，这可是个好东西，它真的有这么灵？
t_en: Oh wow, that's really awesome. Is it really all that effective?

t_ch: 这，你们这……
t_en: Is that... Is that right?

t_ch: 跟你讲，这是我第五次重考，总算是过了。
t_en: I tell you what, this is my fifth time taking the test and I finally passed.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，大哥，五次比平均水平要少了，恭喜你啊！
t_en: Good work, big bro. That's still below the average retest records, so congrats!

t_ch: 可不是，那些教章的石板都快把我家桌子压垮了！花了几个月去学去记，第五次的成绩还是压线过的……
t_en: Right? Those rules carved into the stone tablets nearly crushed my desk! All these months of hard work, and I finally made it on my fifth try, though by a tiny margin...

t_ch: 不提了，过了就是过了，现在想想，努力取得收获，值得。哎，兄弟你当时考了几次？
t_en: Whatever. It's a solid pass after all. Looking back now, it was worth it to work hard for that. *sigh* How many times did you have to take the test, bro?

t_ch: 我没考，有天我路过这里，闲的没事丢了块土给它们，有一只居然说我穿的太蠢，气得我差点跟它们打起来。
t_en: Never done it. I was walking by here one day, bored out of my skull, and chucked a lump of dirt at them. One of them said I dressed like a clown and I blew my stack. We almost came to blows there and then.

t_ch: 正好被我现在的上司看到，他就把我招进来了。
t_en: And my current boss just so happened to see that, and hired me on the spot.

t_ch: 还不够，客人要求「能击碎盔甲」，需要很高韧性，淬火前混一些靛钢进去。
t_en: It's not enough. The customer wants it to be able to "break armor." It needs to be highly durable. Mix some indigo steel in before quenching it.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 下一个订单，定制，瑟希斯权能，锤子。
t_en: Next order. Custom-made Cerces' authority, hammer.

t_ch: 木制的是吧，记得顺着纹理裁切，让他们祷告时能省些功夫，交给西斯了。再下一把是…尼卡多利权能的…我吃不住，杜，你行吗？
t_en: Made from wood eh? Remember to cut along the grain, so they save some trouble during prayers. It's up to Sisk now. The next piece is... Nikador's authority... I can't do this. Tuu, do me a favor?

t_ch: 嗯。…记得，提醒客人。
t_en: Okay... Remember, do remind the customer.

t_ch: 当然，「不能靠祈祷，而是需要用脏话辱骂泰坦，然后躲掉凭空出现的攻击」…真是莫名其妙。
t_en: Of course, "One can't rely on prayers alone, but needs to spit foul words to curse the Titans, then avoid attacks that appear out of thin air" ...This is absurd.

t_ch: 刻法勒啊！这些可爱的小东西们真是它最美妙的造物！
t_en: Holy Kephale! These adorable little things are really its prettiest creations!

t_ch: 小奇美拉是吧，严格来说它们不算是刻法勒造物…不过可爱这点我倒是同意。
t_en: The little chimeras eh? Strictly speaking, they're not Kephale's creations... but I do agree that they're adorable.

t_ch: 可它们长得确实像刻法勒！你瞧那些多变的外观，多么「全世」啊！我传信石版的记忆区已经被各种奇美拉的绘像装满了，等下就打包发到我们的辩论场去。
t_en: But they look just like Kephale! Look at their variety of designs, such a spitting image of Kephale! My teleslate's memory zone is full of all kinds of chimera-related drawings. I'll compress them and send them to our Forum later.

t_ch: 唉，羡慕你们。我…没法靠近它们。
t_en: *sigh* I envy you all. I... can't go near them.

t_ch: 不会吧，你难道怕生？哦，哦！我知道了，是不是过敏？听说因为它们所包含的生物信息繁杂，这种事还挺常发生的…
t_en: Huh, are you afraid of unknown creatures? Ooh, ooh, I know, you're allergic to them? I hear it's because they have a complicated genome, making the allergy of yours fairly common...

t_ch: 不…不是，是它们…对我过敏。唉……
t_en: N... No, they're... allergic to me. *sigh*

t_ch: 泰坦眷属的战争没有止息，集齐火种的时刻也还很遥远，而我们呢？每天就在铺子里打铁！说真的，我已经不知道这种一成不变的生活有什么意义了。
t_en: The Titankin's war never ceases to an end. The instant when all Coreflames are collected is still far away. And us? We're hammering iron all day at the smithy! Honestly, I have no idea what the whole point is of living this monotonous life.

t_ch: 那是因为你最近忙过头了。听说过劳逸结合吗？适度的休息会让你对工作再次产生激情。
t_en: That's because your schedule has been too hectic recently. Heard of work-life balance? Taking timely breaks will invigorate your passion for work.

t_ch: 你说得轻巧，误了订单，战士们少了装备，那可是要命！
t_en: Easier said than done. If orders are delayed, the soldiers will have to fight unarmed. Lives would be truly on the line!

t_ch: 那反过头来想，咱们的工作能够切实帮到战士们，这不是个很好的意义吗？至少我就为此而活，两位客人，你们说是吧？
t_en: Let's think about it on the flip side then: Our work can actually arm the troops and save them. Isn't that a great meaning to life? Well, to my life, at least. What say you, customers?

t_ch: 生活的意义…大祭司大人也是明了此般，才会令我们迁来刻法勒的圣城吧。
t_en: The meaning of life... The High Priest probably understood this as well, which was why he bade us move to Kephale's holy city.

t_ch: 诺杜斯先生…您说得对。能听到这些争论，或许是我们来到奥赫玛后最大的收获。
t_en: Mr. Noldus... You're right. Hearing such discourse is perhaps the greatest boon I've received since we came to Okhema.

t_ch: 听说，元老院出资的探险队回来了，就昨天的事。
t_en: I hear the expeditionary squad that the Council of Elders' funded returned yesterday.

t_ch: 是，刚我在广场那边看到他们了，好像说是发现了些新的矿石，正在和工匠们讨论呢。
t_en: Yes, I saw them at the plaza. They seem to have found some new ores, and are discussing the matter with the craftsmen.

t_ch: 哼，出发前信誓旦旦地说能找到应对黑潮的方法，结果就带了些破石头回来？
t_en: Hmph, they solemnly swore to find a way to counter the black tide before they left, and now they return with a bunch of useless stones?

t_ch: 行啦，又不是你探险，就别说风凉话了。城外那种危险的状态，他们七个都能平平安安的回来，还能带回点收获，已经很了不起了好吧。
t_en: All right, all right, not like you're the one exploring, so can it. Danger lurks everywhere outside the city. It's already a feat that the seven of them came back unscathed, with a minor haul in tow.

t_ch: 七个？他们…不是只去了五个人吗？
t_en: Seven? Didn't... only five go?

t_ch: 金血乃泰坦之神体，而黄金裔乃泰坦神授，自然也是金血先于英雄存在。
t_en: Golden blood is the divine body of the Titans, and the Chrysos Heirs are divinely ordained by the Titans. Naturally, the existence of golden blood precedes that of the heroes.

t_ch: 非也！议题并非探讨概念前后，而是名词的生辰。而黄金裔的故事被传颂前，根本无人知晓究竟「何为金血」。
t_en: On the contrary! We're not talking about the before or after of the concept, but the day the words were created. Before the Chrysos Heirs' story was sung in paeans, no one knew what "golden blood" was.

t_ch: 但黄金裔流淌的并非金血，自然金血一词也并非源于黄金裔之事。
t_en: But what courses through the Chrysos Heirs' veins isn't actual blood in the color of gold, therefore they naturally are not the origin of the words "golden blood."

t_ch: 这…浴场图书室中可寻得物证！曾有教章中，黄金裔一词赫然在列，但金血却以其他名词替代…
t_en: You... You can find solid evidence in the bath's library! The Chrysos Heirs were prominently mentioned in scripture, but other terms replaced "golden blood"...

t_ch: 他们在辩什么啊？
t_en: What are they debating?

t_ch: 嘘，好好听着，这议题相当重要。
t_en: Shush. Just listen. That is a very important topic.

t_ch: …说这喂养奇美拉是门学问，然后啊，我就把我前些日子钓的那条胳膊粗的大鱼喂给它，它反而不怎么喜欢。
t_en: ...Caring for a chimera is a science. Then, I fed it the big fish I caught that was as thick as my arm, but it didn't take much of a liking to it.

t_ch: 确实，奇美拉可是很挑的，要是换我拿三天前用木杆钓的那条腰一样粗的鱼去喂，估计它都不愿意吃。
t_en: Yeah, chimeras are picky. If I fed it the fish thick as my waist that I caught three days ago with a wooden rod, I'm guessing it probably wouldn't eat it either.

t_ch: 瞧瞧，没事和奇美拉较什么劲，还是大地兽好养活，喂土就行。可惜就是，我昨天践行时钓的那条水桶一样大的鱼，它不肯吃哦。
t_en: Why are you obsessed with chimeras? Dromases are much easier to keep. You feed them some soil and that's it. Pity, the fish I caught yesterday during the Action Hour was as big as a bucket, but it just wouldn't eat it.

t_ch: 啊？践行时上哪钓的？啊不是，我就问问，我家那奇美拉说不定会喜欢吃点不一样的。
t_en: Eh? Where did you fish during the Action Hour? Ah, I'm just asking. My chimera might just like something different.

t_ch: 快快，讲讲…
t_en: Quick, tell me...

t_ch: 这个浴池可真舒服啊……
t_en: This bath is so comfortable...

t_ch: 只有草药的香味，我已经泡腻了，要不咱们换个吧？
t_en: There's only the smell of herbs, I'm tired of it. Shall we try baths?

t_ch: 行啊，你想去哪个？
t_en: Sure, which one do you want to go to?

t_ch: 听说云石天宫最近在实验新的浴盐，但我忘了具体是哪个池子…要不咱们每个都试试吧！
t_en: I heard the Marmoreal Palace has been experimenting with new bath salts recently, but I forgot which bath it is... Why not try them all to find out?

t_ch: 我觉得可以，就从旁边的这个浴池开始吧！
t_en: Excellent idea. Let's start from this one at the side!

t_ch: 带孙子可比带儿子费事儿多喽……
t_en: Taking care of grandkids is way harder than just taking care of your own kids...

t_ch: 人又娇气，脾气又大，可真难伺候！
t_en: They're squeamish, bad-tempered, and impossible to please!

t_ch: 嗐，这也就是你家那个儿子出息…瞧瞧我家那几个，找不到工作不说，花钱还大手大脚！唉……
t_en: Ack, that's only possible with a good son like yours... Just look at our lot. None of them can find a job, but they spend money like it's going out of fashion! *sigh*...

t_ch: 那老话怎么说的来着？家家有尊难伺候的泰坦……
t_en: How does the saying go? Each family has its own Titanic issue...

t_ch: …不提也罢，不提也罢啊。
t_en: ...Better let that go.

t_ch: 我这天天都来的，你再便宜点呗？
t_en: I'm a regular customer. Can't you give me a better price?

t_ch: 大姐，小本生意，真不能再便宜啦。
t_en: Madam, I run a small business. That price was the best I can do.

t_ch: 欸，哥们儿，不对吧？这茴香不是我俩前天一起进的货吗？大姐也是老主顾了，你不给她便宜便宜？
t_en: Eh, bro, this isn't right. Didn't we purchase this fennel together just two days back? This lady is a regular customer. Can't you give her a discount?

t_ch: …啊？你谁啊，我怎么不认——
t_en: ...Huh? Who are you? I don't know you...

t_ch: 大姐，我是实诚人，卖东西童叟无欺！您看我这肥皂，用传说中的西尔菲翁和顶好的橄榄油手工做的，是黑潮前才有的货哩……
t_en: Madam, I'm a decent merchant who sells quality goods at honest prices! Check this soap. It's handmade with the legendary Silphion and top-notch olive oil. Usually you can only find such good stuff before the black tide...

t_ch: 唉呀，真是心疼啊…多好的东西，这就扔了。
t_en: Oh my, it pains my heart... So many wonderful things are just thrown away. Such a waste.

t_ch: 老奶奶，我能理解你的心情…不过世界运转的底层逻辑，有时候就是「浪费」。
t_en: I understand how you feel... However, the logic that the world operates on is sometimes just "letting things go to waste."
...Huh?

t_ch: 比如说家里有三个小朋友，每个人都想要获得一颗糖。为了不让这些孩子打起来，老奶奶你应该买几颗糖呢？只买三颗，真的够吗？
t_en: For example, if you have three children at home, and each of them wants a piece of candy. To keep them from fighting, how much candy should you buy for them? Will three pieces do the job?

t_ch: …唉呀，你这丫头说话还真有意思。按你的意思，孩子们吃不完的糖，也只能靠我们这些年纪大的努力吃完了吧？
t_en: ...Oh dear, you really have a way with words. But if I follow your advice, guess there will be plenty of leftovers, and the elders would have to finish them all.

t_ch: 老奶奶，糖吃太多了能有什么好处啊？还是得小心预防糖尿病嘛。
t_en: Come on now, nobody should eat that much candy. You don't want to undermine your health with sweets.

t_ch: 没想到在奥赫玛也能享受到这样的手艺…
t_en: Who knew I'd be able to enjoy such a soothing massage in Okhema...

t_ch: 那您可要多享受享受呢！听我的师父说啊，这份手艺是从黄金世末期传下来的，有两千多年的历史呢。
t_en: Then you should enjoy it to your heart's desire! My master told me that these massage skills have been passed down from the end of the Era Chrysea. There's more than two thousand years of history to it.

t_ch: 真的假的？我家乡的城邦，满打满算也只有一千年的历史…奥赫玛的历史原来这么悠久啊。
t_en: Really? With all things considered, the city back in my hometown has only roughly a thousand years of history... Okhema has such a long-standing history.

t_ch: 哈哈，其实大浴场的导览里也有写到哦？
t_en: Haha, actually it's mentioned in the guide at the baths.

t_ch: 「在光历的第三个一百年中，人们在刻法勒脚下建立起聚落，由此诞生了奥赫玛的前身。」
t_en: "In the third century of the Light Calendar, people built settlements at the feet of Kephale. Thus was the precursor of Okhema founded."

t_ch: 今年是光历<unbreak>4931</unbreak>年…这样算来，奥赫玛已经快有五千年了呢！
t_en: This year is Year <unbreak>4931</unbreak> of the Light Calendar... So, Okhema has been around for almost five thousand years!

t_ch: 泡泡这里的热水，把筋骨都泡开，这样会舒服很多。
t_en: Try this hot bath, it relaxes the muscle. It feel very nice.

t_ch: 不了，爷爷，我还是更喜欢冷水浴，这样能让我的身体更强壮。
t_en: No, grandpa — I still prefer my cold baths. They help strengthen my body.

t_ch: 我们好不容易逃到这里，你也该好好享受在永恒之城的日子。
t_en: It wasn't easy for us to make it here, so you should enjoy yourself while you're in the Eternal City.

t_ch: 正因为我们是外来人，我才更要努力。我要加入卫队，保护这座庇护了我们的城市!
t_en: I have to work harder precisely because we're outsiders. I want to join the guard squad and protect our city that gives us shelter!

t_ch: 哎，真是搞不懂你们年轻人，或许是我老了吧……
t_en: *sigh* I really don't understand you youngsters. Perhaps I'm just getting old...

t_ch: 兄弟，你注意到没，咱们换班的间隔越来越长了。
t_en: Say, bro, you ever noticed that the time between shifts has been getting longer and longer...

t_ch: 我听说是因为最近移民增多，抽调了些人过去维持秩序，人手就不太够了。
t_en: I heard that the number of migrants has been increasing, so some of our people were dispatched there to maintain order, leaving us understaffed.

t_ch: 没办法，听说外面很多城市都被黑潮吞没了。外面的情况…不太乐观啊，说不定哪天我们也要面对…
t_en: Guess there's no other way. I heard lots of cities out there are devoured by the black tide. The situation outside... is not looking very good. Maybe we'll have to face that down ourselves one day...

t_ch: 哎，莫说这些，兄弟。
t_en: Ack, don't say that, brother.

t_ch: 别多想了，我们好歹在奥赫玛，但愿刻法勒能一直保佑我们……
t_en: There's no need to stress yourself out. After all, we're in Okhema. May Kephale protect us...

t_ch: 现在的袍子真不行，我昨天逛了半天，那些所谓的最新设计，我真的看不过眼，太丑了……
t_en: Robes these days are just terrible. I was out shopping all day yesterday, but none of these so-called new designs pleased my eye. They were all hideous...

t_ch: 主要是版型问题，滚边堆砌一堆元素没用，版型那么差，谁穿啊。
t_en: It's mainly the issue with the design. There's no point in piling up all that embroidered stitches if the style is bad. Who wants to wear that?

t_ch: 没办法，你们没发现吗？他们所谓的新「设计」，都是捡阿格莱雅玩剩下的元素东拼西凑。
t_en: No way. Haven't you noticed? Their so-called new "design" is just a hodgepodge of Aglaea's cutting room scraps.

t_ch: 要我说，当初就该求阿格莱雅女士多设计几件，永恒之城需要她的永恒之美。
t_en: If you ask me, we should've asked Lady Aglaea to design more pieces when we had the chance. The Eternal City needs her eternal beauty.

t_ch: 嘬嘬嘬！宝宝！你是一块蓝莓白巧大蛋糕！
t_en: *smooch* Oh my sweetie! Aren't you just a huge slice of blueberry white chocolate cake?

t_ch: 阿米库斯，够了，快住手，别再乱亲那头大地兽了，你没看到它都快急眼了吗？
t_en: Amycus, that's enough! Stop that, stop kissing that dromas. Can't you see it's getting angry?

t_ch: 住手，年轻人！大地兽不是玩具，你再这样我就要请你离开了。
t_en: Stop it, youngster! A dromas is not a plaything. If you keep this up, I'll have to ask you to leave.

t_ch: 阿米库斯，你得学会和大地兽相处，在外面冒险的时候，很多事情都要指望它们。
t_en: Amycus, you have to learn how to get along with dromases properly. You'll have to rely on them a lot when you're out adventuring.

t_ch: 好吧，抱歉，实在是太可爱了，我就摸最后一次！嘬嘬嘬！
t_en: Fine, I'm sorry. They're just too adorable, let me pat it just one more time! *smooch*

t_ch: …真是伤脑筋，说了又不听。
t_en: ...What a headache. He never really listens.

t_ch: 这池子里的水，你有试过偷偷喝两口吗？
t_en: The pool water here... Have you ever tried sneaking a sip of it?

t_ch: …啊这，这…这不太好吧，这池子好歹是我们泡过的啊…我怎么下得去嘴？
t_en: ...Ah, th—that's... not a good idea. The pools here are where we bathe... How could I ever drink that?

t_ch: 别紧张，我只是提醒你别乱喝。因为之前在水里加酵母，就是法吉娜在饮宴上把水变成佳酿的味道，喝了就能排毒之类的，然后喝了一大口……
t_en: Don't worry, I'm just reminding you not to drink it. Someone once added yeast in it and recreated the taste of the ambrosia — one that Phagousa turned water into in the banquet and is supposedly a detoxicant — and took a gulp...

t_ch: 听说是被抬着回去的……
t_en: Anyway, I heard they had to carry that person back home that night...

t_ch: 这个秘方我也听过，但我记得那根本就不是用来喝的，是用来泡澡的。等等，你那什么表情，你该不会也跟着学了吧…
t_en: I've heard of that trick before, it's not a recipe for drinks but for preparing a bath. Waitasec, the look on your face... Don't tell me you did the same thing...

t_ch: …哈…哈，当然没有！你别乱说。
t_en: ...Ha... Ha... Of course not! That's nonsense.

t_ch: 尼莎，你不能在浴池里丢钱币，这么做是很不尊重法吉娜的行为！
t_en: Nyssa, you can't toss coins into the baths. That's disrespecting Phagousa!

t_ch: 怎么会？我明明听大人们说，什么池的硬币会收集起来献给法吉娜。
t_en: Why? I clearly heard the grown-ups saying that all the coins from the pool are collected and offered to Phagousa.

t_ch: …法吉娜在上，这是我对您的敬意。
t_en: ...Phagousa above, I offer this up to you.

t_ch: 那是在神殿里才行，这里是浴池，不能这么乱来。
t_en: That's inside the temple. This is a bath. You can't do that around here.

t_ch: 略略略，我才不听你的，你说了不算！
t_en: Blah-blah-blah. Can't hear you! Blah-blah-blah!

t_ch: 不是，那帮人到底啥毛病？脑袋在浴场泡进水了还是被大地兽踹了？
t_en: Wait, what's wrong with them? Are their heads soaked with bath water or kicked by dromases?

t_ch: 也可能是被门给夹了。活了这么大把年纪，我还是头一回见有人摆摊不做生意，只为了展示的。
t_en: Maybe just bashed against the door frame. At my age, this is my first time seeing someone set up a stall just for display and not for business.

t_ch: 更可恨的是那些人还专挑那些好摊位，各种各样的工艺品齐刷刷摆好几排；结果你上去一问价，好家伙——展示、已出、自留、纪念。
t_en: What's more irritating is that those people even nabbed the good spots. They displayed all kinds of crafts in neat rows, but when you go up and ask for the price, everything is for display only, already sold, reserved for themselves, or their own fond treasure.

t_ch: 怕不是吃饱了撑的…这不是扰乱城内秩序吗？
t_en: Do they have nothing better to do... Isn't this disrupting the order of the city?

t_ch: 可不是吗，我听说已经有摊主气不过去找卫兵举报了，也不知道什么时候能给处理了……
t_en: I'd say so myself. I've heard that some stall owners were so upset that they reported it to the guards already. I hope they'll handle this soon...

t_ch: …所以祭司大人，您真的不考虑……
t_en: ...Lady Priestess, are you sure that you will not consider...

t_ch: 真的真的，比奥赫玛的太阳还真。还有不要再叫我祭司大人了，老婆子我不干这行已经好些年啦。
t_en: Yes, as sure as I know that Okhema has a sun. Also, please stop calling me Lady Priestess. It's been many years since I quit this job.

t_ch: …我不明白，以您的身份地位，就算离开了雅努萨波利斯，也应当受到众人的尊崇敬仰，而不是……
t_en: ...I don't understand. Even if you have left Janusopolis, with your accomplishments and status, you should be respected and admired by all, instead of being...

t_ch: 这么说吧，孩子，你觉得我现在所做的事情，与之前在本质上有什么不同吗？
t_en: Let me put it this way. My child, do you think that what I'm doing now is fundamentally different from what I used to do?

t_ch: 当然不一样！您所身负的，是向凡人传达神谕的伟业——如此高贵的您怎能以「占卜师」这样…这样不体面的身份自贬……
t_en: Of course it's different! You carry the sacred duty of passing prophecies to mortals — how can someone as noble as you be a "soothsayer"... How can you demean yourself like this...

t_ch: 你是这么认为的啊…可我倒是觉得，大祭司也好、占卜师也罢，所做的都不过是在命运洪流之中试图捞取一些名为「未来」的碎片，将它们交于有所希求之人。
t_en: So this is what you think... But to me, even if it's the High Priestess or a soothsayer, all we do is merely attempting to grasp fragments of the "future" amidst the tumultuous current of destiny and offer it to those who seek them.
But...

t_ch: 好啦，回去吧，孩子——别再执着于让我重拾那些无谓的虚名啦。要是真的放心不下我，倒不如有空多来照顾一下老婆子的生意~
t_en: There, there, go now, my child — stop obsessing over having me reclaim those meaningless titles. If you're really concerned about me, just come back often and support my business.

t_ch: 盖亚，你又在浴场闹事了？
t_en: Gaia, did you cause trouble at the baths again?

t_ch: 嘘！小点声。那叫科学研究，懂不懂？
t_en: Hush! Keep your voice down. I did not cause trouble. It was a scientific research, got it?

t_ch: 你非得在浴场做实验吗？
t_en: Do you really have to conduct your experiments at the baths?

t_ch: 当然啦！黄金裔都在那儿嘛。
t_en: Of course! The Chrysos Heirs are all there.

t_ch: 你迟早会惹上大麻烦的……
t_en: You're gonna get yourself into serious trouble sooner or later.

t_ch: 尤利娅，你又在这里发呆！
t_en: Julia, you're daydreaming here again!

t_ch: 我才没发呆，这叫冥想研究！
t_en: I'm not daydreaming. This is called a meditation experiment!

t_ch: 别天真了，树庭这么多学者，吃饭不用钱？研究学问不用钱？没钱怎么做学问？你好歹也体谅下我吧，家里的生活费还是我出的呢。
t_en: Don't be naive. There are so many scholars in the Grove. Their food and studies all require funds. We won't last without money. I'm the one covering our living expenses. Have a heart!

t_ch: 别就知道钱钱钱的好吗，你的短视让瑟希斯蒙羞！
t_en: Can you stop talking about money all the time? Your shortsightedness brings shame to Cerces!

t_ch: 两个月也太久了吧…我追求了她七年，好不容易求婚成功，现在真是一刻钟也等不下去了！
t_en: Two months is too long, surely...? I have been pursuing her for seven years and she finally said yes to my marriage proposal. I simply cannot wait another minute!

t_ch: 我非常理解你的心情，但很抱歉，我最近的行程确实已经排满了。
t_en: I completely understand how you are feeling. But I must apologize, my schedule is completely full.

t_ch: 你理解我？你一个小孩子家家能理解什么！我就不明白了，偌大一个奥赫玛竟然只有你一个司仪，这合理吗！
t_en: You understand how I'm feeling? What could a child possibly understand!? I don't get it. How can there be only one ceremonial officiant in a huge place like Okhema? It's just ridiculous!

t_ch: 因为想成为司仪的话，需要去树庭进修，并通过「敬拜学派」的特殊考核……
t_en: That is because in order to become a ceremonial officiant, you have to train in the Grove and pass a special examination put in place by the Venerationists...

t_ch: 谁问你这个了！真是的，这种破规矩究竟是哪个混蛋定下来的……
t_en: I wasn't asking! Arrrgh, what kind of idiot came up with rules like that...

t_ch: …啧，七年都等了还差这两个月？慢走不送！
t_en: ...Tsk. You've waited seven years and you can't even hold on two more months? You know your way out!

t_ch: 诶呦——我又赢啦！承让了啊兄弟！
t_en: Oh... I win again! You have to stop letting me win, brother!

t_ch: 啧…你这手气也太离谱了，怕不是出千了吧？！
t_en: Tsk... This kind of luck just isn't right. Are you sure you're not cheating!?

t_ch: 哪儿能啊，就咱这运势，还用得着出千？我跟你说，这么多年来我就从来没输过！
t_en: How could I? With luck like mine, why would I need to cheat? Let me tell you, I've played for many years, and I've never once lost!

t_ch: 瞧把你给厉害的…等等，我记得你好像说过，常常会和你们运输队的领班来几局，难道也……
t_en: Look at you getting cocky with a few wins... Wait, I remember you said that you used to play all the time with the head of your transport team. You didn't... No way...

t_ch: 那必须的！不然你以为我平时喝秘酿的钱是哪来的！
t_en: Of course I won! How else do you think I got to pay for the ambrosia?

t_ch: …怪不得你干了这么多年还是个劳工……
t_en: ...No wonder you never got any promotion after all these years...

t_ch: 这就不行了？才跑了几步就累成这副德行？出去别说是我的学生，丢人！
t_en: Is that all you've got? You run a few steps, and you're in such a sorry state already? Don't go telling anyone you're my students, as you'll bring me nothing but shame!

t_ch: …老…呼…老师…我们已经、绕着云石天宫…跑了二…二十圈了……
t_en: ...S—Sir... Phew... We've—We've already done... t—twenty... laps around the... Marmoreal Palace...

t_ch: 啧，行吧，那就全体休息一刻钟，然后再来二十组俯卧撑。
t_en: Pfft, fine. Take a quint, then finish twenty sets of push-ups!

t_ch: 嘶——老、老师，人家讲学都是收钱…您、您是要命啊！
t_en: Ahh... S—Sir... They normally take their students' money for the lectures... N—Not their lives...

t_ch: 这才哪到哪，啊？就你们这小体格子，再不加紧锻炼，小心连学院大门都进不去！
t_en: What, too much to handle already? Ah, if you weaklings don't start taking training seriously, you might never make it into the academy!

t_ch: 不是…为啥就咱们这么特殊啊，也没见其它学派有过什么入门考核…那个投石索也太难扔了吧……
t_en: That's not what I mean... Why do we have to do this? I never saw any entrance exams for the other Schools... And that sling is way too heavy to swing...

t_ch: 当然是因为——只有强健的体魄，才能承载强韧的灵魂；只有强韧的灵魂，才能触及纯粹的真理！
t_en: It goes without saying, a powerful soul must be paired with a strong body, or it will never reach the pure truths!

t_ch: 但我们的根本目的不还是学知识吗…就您这个训练强度，怕不是要把我们都培养成竞技场英雄……
t_en: But we came here to learn... With such intense training, I'm afraid we would become arena champions instead...

t_ch: 我再重申一遍！我们的肉体乃是灵魂的容器，就像作物成长须得倚赖泥土的滋养，灵魂的完满也离不开肉体的丰盈——
t_en: I will say this one more time! Our bodies are the vessels of our souls! Just as the growth of crops relies on the soil's nourishment, the nourishment of the soul cannot be separated from the abundance of the body...

t_ch: 若是一味地追求丰产而不停耕种，却疏于对土地加以养护，那么迟早有一天，我们赖以生存的根基就会被榨干。
t_en: If you simply pursue abundant harvests and spend all your time reaping without nurturing the land, one day the very foundation of our survival will dry up and wither.

t_ch: 灵魂在得以质变擢升之前，就因肉体的衰败而枯萎——这将是何等的悲剧！
t_en: For the soul to wither and decline before achieving elevation because of a debilitated body... What a tragedy that would be!

t_ch: 即便您这么说，也……
t_en: Even so...

t_ch: 很好，既然你如此有精神，待会儿再多加五组吧。
t_en: Very well! As you've got the energy to argue, finish another five sets!

t_ch: 啊？！不是——别啊老师，我知道错了！
t_en: Huh!? No — Please, Sir, I was wrong!

t_ch: 孩子，你怎么又私自闯入这里？幸亏是我先发现了你，如果撞见你的是卫兵……
t_en: Child, what are you doing sneaking in here again? You're lucky it was me that found you and not one of the guards...

t_ch: 欧弥利亚婶婶，我发誓这次真的不一样了！就在今日，刻法勒已赐予我第二个征兆，虽然我答应他不能说…我是命中注定的十二黄金裔之一，你要相信我！
t_en: Auntie Aumilia, I promise this time is different! Kephale has given me a second sign today. Though I promised them I wouldn't say... I am definitely one of the twelve fated Chrysos Heirs. You have to believe me!

t_ch: 哎，伊克西翁，我理解你担心城邦的安危，希望尽自己所能。但万一你失败了呢？那金血，凡人碰了可是要殒命啊！
t_en: Ah, Ixion... I know that you are concerned for the safety of the city, and you want to do what you can. But what if you are unsuccessful? If a mortal comes into contact with the golden blood, they will lose their lives!

t_ch: 不可能，我就是黄金裔！况且，自我决心踏上这条道路，我便无所畏惧，就像神话中真正的英雄一样。我一定会拯救奥赫玛的诸位……
t_en: No way. I am a Chrysos Heir! Plus, I have decided to take this path for myself. So, I have no fear! Just like those heroes in the myths! I will save everyone in Okhema...

t_ch: …你有考虑过你的姐姐吗？她已经在战争中失去了父母，失去了兄长。如果你也将自己的性命置于危险中，你至少应当先与她道别，再采取行动。
t_en: ...Have you thought about your sister? She has already lost her father and mother to battle. She lost her older brother too. If you are going to put your life in danger, you should at least bid her farewell before you act.

t_ch: …我确实没有告诉她是今天。我再等等姐姐，谢谢你，欧弥利亚婶婶。
t_en: ...You're right. I didn't tell her today was the day. I will wait for her. Thank you, Auntie Aumilia.

t_ch: 所以您说，若是云层厚积，且永夜频闪，是要「下雨」。
t_en: So, you're saying thick clouds and evernight flashes are a sign of "rain"?

t_ch: 正是，我亲爱的学生。
t_en: That is correct, my dear student.

t_ch: 所以，冒险者们会在此之前备好雨具。
t_en: So, adventurers will prepare rain gear in advance?

t_ch: 不错的联想。
t_en: A fine inference.

t_ch: 所以，我可以在那之前大量购入，然后涨价卖给冒险者们，这样就能大赚一笔，随后攒够利衡币搬去云崖住。
t_en: So, I can buy in a huge amount of rain gear beforehand and sell them to adventurers for an inflated price? Then I can make a fortune. I'll have enough Balance Coins to move to Dawncloud.

t_ch: …差点忘了，你压根不是来求学的，唉。
t_en: ...I almost forgot that you weren't here to learn... *sigh*

t_ch: 我好羡慕你们！生来就受到吉奥里亚的赐福，拥有强大的力量，这样才能保护身边的人！
t_en: I envy you all so much! Being born with the benediction of Georios and having amazing strength means that you're able to protect everyone around you!

t_ch: 我做饭，也很厉害。要尝尝吗？
t_en: My cooking, also very good. Want to have a taste?

t_ch: 啊，你喜欢做饭，好浪费…为什么我就不是山之民呢？
t_en: Ah, you like cooking? What a waste... Why can't I be a Mountain Dweller?
